projects.name: Mid-Range Kitchen Remodel
projects.description: Replace laminate countertops with quartz, replace cabinet fronts (keep boxes), new undermount sink and faucet, tile backsplash, new appliances (range, dishwasher, microwave hood), luxury vinyl plank flooring.

diy.partialDIY: Backsplash, painting, hardware swaps are DIY-able. Countertop, cabinet fronts, appliances, plumbing best left to contractors.

roiData.notes: Cleveland market ROI for kitchen remodels runs slightly below national average. Mid-range remodels tend to outperform upscale in this price band — buyers here value function over luxury finishes.

projects.name: Master Bath Refresh
projects.description: Replace vanity and fixtures, re-tile shower surround (repair failed grout first), new toilet, paint, updated lighting and mirrors. Keeping existing layout.

diy.partialDIY: Painting, mirror/lighting swap, toilet replacement DIY-able. Tile work and vanity plumbing best left to pros.

roiData.notes: Bathroom remodels have strong ROI in this market. Master bath update is especially impactful for buyers. Cosmetic refresh (vs. full gut) keeps costs down and ROI percentage high.

projects.name: Basement Finishing — Rec Room
projects.description: Frame and drywall unfinished half of basement (~500 sqft), add egress window, install LVP flooring, recessed lighting, electrical outlets. Not including a wet bar or bathroom.

diy.partialDIY: Painting and flooring installation can be DIY. Framing, electrical, and egress window require permits and are best left to contractors.

roiData.notes: IMPORTANT: Do not begin basement finishing until water intrusion issue is fully resolved and waterproofing is complete. Finishing over an active moisture problem will destroy the investment. Resolve foundation issue first — budget $3K–$8K for that before this project.

projects.name: Deck Rebuild
projects.description: Full deck rebuild — proper ledger flashing, pressure-treated frame, composite decking boards (Trex or equivalent), code-compliant railing and stair handrail. Same footprint (~12x16).

diy.partialDIY: Not recommended for DIY — ledger attachment and railing code compliance requires permits and experience.

roiData.notes: Decks have above-average ROI in the Midwest — outdoor space is valued. Composite decking adds to upfront cost but appeals to buyers who don't want annual sealing. Rebuild (vs. repair) is the right call given ledger issue.

projects.name: Interior Paint — Full House
projects.description: Professional paint throughout — all walls, ceilings, trim. Neutral palette (agreeable gray / warm white). 3 bed, 2.5 bath, living, dining, kitchen, basement stairwell.

diy.partialDIY: Fully DIY-able. Cost drops to ~$800–$1,200 in materials. Time investment is ~3–4 weekends.

roiData.notes: Interior paint is one of the highest-ROI projects available — often returns more than cost. Neutral tones appeal to broadest buyer pool. Best bang for buck if budget is limited.

recommendedSequence: 1. Resolve roof, electrical panel, and basement water intrusion (non-negotiable — required before anything else)
2. Interior paint (highest ROI, sets tone for everything else)
3. Master bath refresh (strong ROI, relatively contained project)
4. Deck rebuild (outdoor living, above-average ROI in this market)
5. Kitchen remodel (largest investment, do after living in the home)
6. Basement finishing (only after waterproofing confirmed complete)
